lumin_hint.name: Lumielle
lumin_hint.dialogue: Look toward the light — it hides in every line. Ask yourself what the author keeps returning to.

lumin_lore.name: Starwarden
lumin_lore.dialogue: We Lumin were born from the first dawn, before the Realm had words. Every sunrise is a new chapter.

lumin_encouragement.name: Brightjoy
lumin_encouragement.dialogue: Your progress shines SO brightly we can see it from the sky! Keep glowing, young scholar!

lumin_easter.name: Flicker
lumin_easter.dialogue: I once swallowed a firefly whole. I glowed for a week. 10 out of 10 — would recommend.

thornkin_hint.name: Sylvex the Seeker
thornkin_hint.dialogue: Psst! Looking for theme? Ask yourself: what does the character LEARN by the end? That's your clue, young traveler.

thornkin_lore.name: Elder Mossbark
thornkin_lore.dialogue: The Verdant Vale has stood since the first story was told. Every leaf here holds a tale. The Warden watches... always.

thornkin_encouragement.name: Bramblejoy
thornkin_encouragement.dialogue: YOU ARE DOING AMAZING! The Vale believes in you! Go go go! WOOOO!

thornkin_easter.name: Twig
thornkin_easter.dialogue: I found this scroll but I cannot read it. It says... 'Ziν aℜe iη sℜv'... I think it might be a recipe for acorn soup?

gravenborn_hint.name: Stonewhisper
gravenborn_hint.dialogue: Each piece of evidence should support your claim like a stone supports a wall. Find three and the wall stands.

gravenborn_lore.name: Chiselmark
gravenborn_lore.dialogue: The Stone Kingdoms were carved long before memory. Every wall holds a secret. I have read most of them.

gravenborn_encouragement.name: Bouldercheer
gravenborn_encouragement.dialogue: You are SOLID. You are STRONG. You are doing great, little scholar! Nothing can crumble your effort!

gravenborn_easter.name: Pebble
gravenborn_easter.dialogue: If I stand very still, they think I am a rock. I have stood still for three years. No one has noticed.

tideweaver_hint.name: Coralwhisper
tideweaver_hint.dialogue: Words drift like currents. Look for context clues nearby to anchor the meaning before you guess.

tideweaver_lore.name: Riptide Elder
tideweaver_lore.dialogue: The Depths were shaped by pressure over ages. Even the darkest trench holds life — and answers.

tideweaver_encouragement.name: Bubblejoy
tideweaver_encouragement.dialogue: YOU'RE MAKING WAVES! Keep swimming, scholar! The surface — and your goal — is just ahead!

tideweaver_easter.name: Gloop
tideweaver_easter.dialogue: I once mistook a jellyfish for a hat. It was a confusing afternoon for both of us. The jellyfish was also confused.

briarfolk_hint.name: Thornwhisper
briarfolk_hint.dialogue: Compare two texts like two paths through the woods — notice what changes and what stays the same.

briarfolk_lore.name: Rootkeeper
briarfolk_lore.dialogue: The Thornwood remembers every story told beneath its canopy. Every root is a thread to the past.

briarfolk_encouragement.name: Leafdance
briarfolk_encouragement.dialogue: GROWING SO TALL! You've rooted your knowledge deep! Keep branching out — the sky is waiting!

briarfolk_easter.name: Snag
briarfolk_easter.dialogue: Last Tuesday I tried to walk through a door and got stuck for six hours. The door wins. The door always wins.

embersoul_hint.name: Cinderveil
embersoul_hint.dialogue: Can't find the evidence? Keep reading — the author will light the way soon. Follow the smoke.

embersoul_lore.name: Ashkeeper
embersoul_lore.dialogue: The Hollows were once bright and burning. Now only the embers remain to remember what the fire once was.

embersoul_encouragement.name: Sparkjoy
embersoul_encouragement.dialogue: YOU'RE ON FIRE! Figuratively! Possibly literally! Your hard work is blazing brighter every day!

embersoul_easter.name: Smolderpot
embersoul_easter.dialogue: I collect shadows. I have 47 shadows. My favorite is the shadow from a donut. It has a hole, you see.

voltari_hint.name: Stormscribe
voltari_hint.dialogue: Need to compare sources? List what they agree on first, then find what only one of them says.

voltari_lore.name: Thunderpeak
voltari_lore.dialogue: The Stormspire was raised in the final age by those who wished to touch the clouds — and succeeded.

voltari_encouragement.name: Zappyjoy
voltari_encouragement.dialogue: ELECTRIC EFFORT! You're basically a walking lightning bolt of genius right now! CRACKLE CRACKLE!

voltari_easter.name: Crackle
voltari_easter.dialogue: I once counted all the lightning bolts in a storm. There were seventeen thousand. I may have miscounted by one.

land1.title: The Scribe's Calling
land1.prompt: Choose a character from Unit 1 who overcomes an obstacle. Write an essay analyzing how the character's response to that obstacle reveals a theme of the story. Use evidence from the text to support your analysis.
checklist: I stated a clear theme supported by the text
I analyzed how the character's actions reveal the theme
I used at least two pieces of text evidence
I wrote in complete sentences with correct punctuation

land2.title: The Scribe's Calling
land2.prompt: Write an informational article about one of the art movements or historical events from Unit 2. Include key details from at least two texts to explain why this event or movement was important.
checklist: I introduced my topic clearly
I used details and facts from at least two unit texts
I organized my information logically
I wrote in complete sentences with correct punctuation

land3.title: The Scribe's Calling
land3.prompt: Write an original legend that explains something about water — how a river was formed, why the ocean is salty, or where rain comes from. Use figurative language including at least one metaphor or simile in your legend.
checklist: I wrote a legend that explains something about water
I included a clear beginning, middle, and end
I used at least one metaphor or simile
I wrote in complete sentences with correct punctuation

land4.title: The Scribe's Calling
land4.prompt: Write a letter to the editor stating your opinion on this question: What is the most important survival skill a person can have? Use evidence from at least two texts in Unit 4 to support your opinion.
checklist: I stated my opinion clearly in the opening
I supported my opinion with evidence from at least two unit texts
I addressed a possible opposing view
I wrote a strong concluding statement

land5.title: The Scribe's Calling
land5.prompt: Write a short persuasive speech about this question: Why is it important to stand up against injustice, even when it is dangerous? Use evidence from Unit 5 texts to support your argument.
checklist: I opened with a strong hook that grabs the audience
I stated my opinion and supported it with unit text evidence
I used persuasive language throughout
I ended with a powerful call to action

land6.title: The Scribe's Calling
land6.prompt: Write one chapter of an informational book about communication. Choose one form of communication from Unit 6 and explain how it works, why it matters, and what the world would be like without it. Use details from at least two unit texts.
checklist: I introduced my topic with an engaging opening
I included facts and details from at least two unit texts
I used headings or organized sections if helpful
I wrote a conclusion that wraps up the chapter
